confirmed_successful.effects: Stabilises layered structure; suppresses Li/Ni mixing; reduces capacity slightly; improves thermal stability
key_references: Cho et al., Electrochem. Solid-State Lett. 2001

confirmed_successful.effects: Suppresses phase transitions at high voltage; reduces cation mixing; aliovalent (charge compensation via Ni2+/Ni3+ ratio)
key_references: Kim et al., J. Electrochem. Soc. 2017

confirmed_successful.effects: Pillaring effect in Li layer; reduces Li/Ni disorder at low concentrations; capacity penalty at high concentrations
key_references: Huang et al., J. Power Sources 2018

confirmed_successful.effects: Forms W-rich grain boundary phase; enhances rate capability; very low solubility in bulk lattice
key_references: Kim et al., Adv. Energy Mater. 2018

confirmed_successful.effects: Stabilises structure during cycling; reduces voltage fade; improves cycle life
key_references: Schipper et al., J. Electrochem. Soc. 2017

confirmed_successful.effects: Strong TM-O bond stabilisation; suppresses oxygen loss at high voltage
Similar to Ta; stabilises high-voltage operation; reduces impedance growth
key_references: Sun et al., ACS Energy Lett. 2020

confirmed_successful.effects: Multi-valent; stabilises structure; may segregate to grain boundaries
Earth-abundant Co replacement; increases Li/Ni disorder at high concentrations; voltage penalty
key_references: Zheng et al., J. Power Sources 2016

confirmed_successful.effects: Multi-valent; can participate in redox; moderate structural stabilisation
Good radius match to Co3+; stabilises layered structure; toxicity concern
Very small ion; may partially occupy tetrahedral sites; strengthens TM-O bonds
key_references: Park et al., J. Power Sources 2018

confirmed_successful.effects: Similar electronic config to Al; stabilises layered structure; suppresses phase transition

confirmed_limited.notes: Limited reports; relatively large ion for octahedral site; cost-prohibitive
Limited reports; similar to Zr but heavier and more expensive
Large ionic radius limits solubility; may segregate to grain boundaries
Substitutes on Li site, not TM site; pillaring effect; limited solubility

confirmed_failed.failure_mode: Phase separation above ~2% concentration; very large ionic radius
